message:
fix(share): resolve relative SQLite paths to absolute candidates across all share entry points

When the DATABASE_URL contains a relative path that doesn't exist at the
literal relative location but does exist as an absolute path (common when
the URL was stored from a different working directory), share operations
failed with "cannot open snapshot" errors.

- Add resolve_share_sqlite_path() helper that checks whether a relative
  path exists; if not, tries prepending "/" and returns the absolute
  candidate if it exists. Explicit relative paths (./foo, ../foo),
  absolute paths, and :memory: are passed through unchanged.
- Apply resolve_share_sqlite_path at every SQLite open-site: bundle.rs
  (bundle_attachments, export_viewer_data), deploy.rs (quick_check,
  validate_agent_mail_schema), finalize.rs (open_conn), scope.rs
  (apply_project_scope), scrub.rs (scrub_snapshot), snapshot.rs
  (create_sqlite_snapshot), static_render.rs (render_static_site)
- Remove unused sqlmodel_core::Value import from static_render.rs
- Add tests: resolve prefers existing absolute, keeps explicit relative,
  snapshot uses absolute candidate, static render uses absolute candidate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
